decks.notes: The key concept of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Landstill utilizes a combination of control elements such as Counterspell, Mana Leak, and Swords to Plowshares to manage threats while also generating card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction and Accumulated Knowledge. By maintaining a strong board presence with efficient creature lands like Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave, this deck aims to establish a dominant position on the battlefield and gradually wear down the opponent's resources to secure victory.

The deck aims to utilize the combo of Tinker and Fling to quickly mill the opponent's library. Phyrexian Devourer is used with Altar of Dementia to dump cards into the opponent's graveyard, setting up for a lethal Fling. Defense Grid helps protect the combo from disruption. Oath of Druids can help speed up the deck by putting creatures into play. The mana base is designed for speed and consistency, allowing for the combo to be executed efficiently. The deck is built to win through a quick and powerful combo finish.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to control the board with disruptive spells and creatures. Using Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures into play while hindering opponent's resources with Sphere of Resistance and land destruction. The aim is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures like Terravore and disrupt their game plan with spells like Pyroclasm. The versatility of the deck lies in its ability to adapt to different strategies while maintaining control over the game.

The deck revolves around rapidly assembling the combo of Phyrexian Devourer and Fling to deal direct damage to the opponent, with Tinker serving as a way to search for the necessary pieces. The deck also includes mana acceleration and disruption spells to protect the combo, aiming to win the game quickly before the opponent can establish their own game plan. The deck is heavy on artifacts, utilizing cards like Altar of Dementia and Defense Grid to further support the combo strategy.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a horde of aggressive goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to quickly deploy threats. Cards like Goblin Ringleader help maintain card advantage, while Goblin Warchief and other tribal support cards help reduce the cost of playing out the goblins. The deck also includes some removal options like Gempalm Incinerator and Mogg Fanatic to clear the way for attacks. With a mix of speed, power, and synergy, this goblin aggro deck aims to outpace and overrun the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board through countering spells, removal, and using cards like Standstill to slow down the opponent's game plan. Landstill relies on using lands as resources while leveraging powerful control spells such as Counterspell and Mana Leak to disrupt the opponent's strategy. By continuously denying the opponent resources and maintaining card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction, this deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition. Additionally, cards like Decree of Justice and Wrath of God provide additional answers to threats on the board.

The Mono Black Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand and board presence through cards like Duress, Cabal Therapy, and Hypnotic Specter. It also utilizes The Rack and Cursed Scroll to deal damage to the opponent's life total. Wasteland and Mishra's Factory provide disruption and enable the deck to control the board. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by using cards like Graveborn Muse and Skeletal Scrying to draw cards and maintain card advantage. Ultimately, the deck aims to control the game and win through attrition.

The deck focuses on controlling the game and surviving through various means such as discarding opponent's key cards, sacrificing creatures for value, and tutoring for specific creatures with Survival of the Fittest. It utilizes a mix of creatures and spells to maintain control of the board and out value the opponent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up its own resources and ultimately overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures. The synergy between the various creatures and spells allows for flexibility in dealing with different situations and adapting to the opponent's strategy.

The focus of this deck is to control the game through counterspells, removal, and hand disruption while utilizing Psychatog as a threat to win the game. The deck aims to efficiently draw cards, control the board, and disrupt the opponent's plans by utilizing the graveyard with cards like Deep Analysis and Burning Wish. With a mix of control elements and a powerful finisher in Psychatog, this deck can outlast opponents and secure victories through resource management and strategic play.

This deck relies on a quick and aggressive strategy using Goblins to overwhelm the opponent. With cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief to accelerate and pump up the creatures, along with removal cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander to deal with threats, this deck aims to win fast and efficiently. With a mix of creature tutoring, ramp, and disruption, it aims to maintain board control while dealing direct damage to the opponent. Forest and Karplusan Forest help ensure consistent mana production while utilizing lands like Rishadan Port and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on using cards like Ill-Gotten Gains and other combo pieces to set up a powerful win condition. It utilizes cards that can manipulate the graveyard, generate mana quickly, and search for key pieces to execute its combo. By efficiently cycling through the deck and casting spells at the right time, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory through a strategic and precise gameplay approach in the premodern format.

The main idea of this deck is to aggressively utilize the Madness and Threshold mechanics to quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and efficient spells. By discarding cards with Madness to generate value and fill up the graveyard for Threshold, the deck can consistently put out threats and protect them with counterspells like Circular Logic and Daze. Survival of the Fittest serves as a versatile tutor to find key creatures like Arrogant Wurm or utility creatures like Gilded Drake. The deck's mana base is carefully crafted to provide a balanced mix of colors for casting spells efficiently.

The central idea of this deck is to quickly swarm the board with low-cost creatures and sacrifice them for various effects, such as generating tokens or disrupting your opponent's hand. The deck aims to be aggressive and put pressure on the opponent early on, using cards like Dark Ritual to accelerate into powerful plays. With a focus on sacrificing creatures for value, this Mono Black Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of speed and disruption, denying them the resources they need to mount a defense.

The main focus of this Mono Black Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through hand disruption cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy, along with creature removal like Snuff Out and Smother. The deck also aims to apply pressure with efficient threats like Hypnotic Specter and Ravenous Rats while utilizing The Rack as a win condition. With a mana denial strategy using Wasteland and Mishra's Factory, this deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind out a victory through resource management and efficient use of removal spells.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing the powerful Oath of Druids card to quickly bring out the Terravore creature while disrupting your opponent's gameplan with disruption spells like Swords to Plowshares and Sphere of Resistance. The deck also includes land destruction cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port to further slow down the opponent's strategy. The overall strategy is to control the board, disrupt the opponent, and quickly establish a strong presence with the Terravore creature.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game and survive by using cards like Winter Orb, Opposition, and Survival of the Fittest. The deck relies on utilizing powerful creatures like Genesis, Deranged Hermit, and Palinchron to maintain board presence and out-value the opponent. With a strong mana base and a mix of creature removal, card advantage, and efficient threats, this control-survival archetype aims to create a strategic advantage and win through incremental advantage over time.
